items.title: Stop Overpaying for EBS I The Keys to AWS Optimization | S16 E3

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This livestream presents practical Amazon EBS cost optimization strategies, including identifying overprovisioned volumes, right-sizing storage, leveraging the latest EBS volume types, and removing waste from unused snapshots and outdated or inefficient volumes. It includes real-world examples, demos of monitoring tools for storage utilization, and actionable techniques to cut EBS spending without sacrificing performance.
bullets: Identify and right‑size overprovisioned EBS volumes to reduce costs.
Leverage the latest EBS volume types to better match performance needs with cost efficiency.
Eliminate waste by cleaning up unused snapshots, outdated volumes, and inefficient configurations.
Demonstrations of monitoring tools to track storage utilization.
Real‑world examples and actionable strategies to lower EBS spending without sacrificing performance.
takeaways: Right‑sizing volumes and choosing appropriate EBS types can materially reduce storage spend while preserving required performance.
Removing unused snapshots and outdated volumes yields immediate, low‑effort savings.
Monitoring storage utilization is essential for ongoing cost governance and preventing future waste.
Practical, demonstrable techniques enable engineering teams to implement savings without performance tradeoffs.

items.title: Evan Bates Praises Figure Skating's Next Generation

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Evan Bates praises the next generation of figure skaters, saying the sport’s future has never looked brighter. The short Team USA clip carries a supportive, optimistic tone and points viewers to Team USA’s social channels.
bullets: Evan Bates commends the next generation of figure skating.
Message: the future of figure skating “has never looked brighter.”
Published by Team USA on YouTube (2026-02-05).

Cross-post the clip to the listed Team USA channels to maintain consistent messaging and reach.
Feature Evan Bates’ quote in marketing/press to highlight athlete support for the next generation.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description… In 2015, full-time contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York intending to build Airbnb properties. Over the next three years he commuted from Connecticut on weekends to build an A-frame cabin himself, added more cabins over time, and as of September 2025 the property has generated over $2.1 million.
bullets: Bought a five-acre property in upstate New York in 2015 to develop Airbnb rentals.
Chris, a full-time contractor, built an A-frame cabin himself over three years via weekend commutes from Connecticut.
Additional cabins were added to the property over time.
By September 2025 the property had brought in over $2.1 million.
Project evolved from a single DIY build into a multi-cabin rental portfolio.
takeaways: Small parcels of land can be developed into high-revenue assets, so land-use opportunity assessments matter for infrastructure planning.
Phased, owner-led development models indicate that demand and load can grow over time—plan capacity and scalability accordingly.
Rural or remote short-term rental projects emphasize the need for reliable local utilities and connectivity when evaluating site suitability.
Long development-to-return timelines (multi-year build and revenue accrual) should be factored into long-term asset and grid planning.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This KPMG ON AIR podcast (Monika Palmowska and Tomasz Regulski) explains how to view operational changes—like moving production, distribution, teams or contracts—through the lens of transfer pricing and valuations, highlighting recognition, reporting obligations and common valuation errors. It stresses the importance of timely documentation, covers TPR and MDR reporting duties, and ends with practical examples and a checklist for companies.
bullets: Defines restructuring in transfer pricing context and how to identify material changes (EBIT analysis).
Explains documentation requirements for restructurings and why prompt preparation matters.
Details reporting obligations, including TPR and MDR.
Covers the role of valuations, frequent mistakes, and pitfalls when transferring contracts or employees.
Provides classic examples (relocating production/distribution) and a practical checklist for firms.
takeaways: Plan transfer-pricing and valuation work early when considering operational moves to avoid reporting gaps.
Assess materiality via EBIT and document decisions to support tax positions and defend against inquiries.
Include TPR and MDR reporting checks in project timelines for any cross-border or related-party restructuring.
Review valuation assumptions and contract-transfer implications to prevent common valuation and reporting errors.
